resolution.summary: governed commit 改 temporary-index，foreign staged 檔物理排除。

finding: Concurrent captain lanes still contaminate each other through the shared git index: one captain's staged rename set (25 files, ATM-GOV-0101..0123 relocation in the planning repo) was swept wholesale into another captain's unrelated commit 15eebaf6 'docs(rft): open follow-up task cards' authored by codex-gpt-5.5. The tree state ended correct by luck, but the commit is mislabeled, attribution is wrong, and the same mechanism can just as easily commit half-finished foreign work.
expectedBehavior: Governed commit paths must never include staged paths outside their own declared bundle/claim scope. Every governed commit should stage into a temporary index (or use git commit --only with its declared path set) so foreign staged files are physically excluded, matching the taskflow close temporary-index fix already shipped for AAF.
evidenceOrRepro: 3KLife commits 15eebaf6 (foreign commit containing this captain's renames) and e8f6908b (follow-up content commit) on 2026-07-17; AAF side repeatedly saw ATM-GOV-0155 lane files appear in this captain's index (ATM_GIT_RECORD_COMMIT_SCOPE_VIOLATION listing packages/cli/src/commands/doctor/run-doctor.ts).
proposedDirection: Extend the temporary-index commit discipline to all governed commit surfaces in both repos (record-commit, git commit --task, planning-repo commit helpers), and add a doctor patrol that flags commits whose file set spans multiple task scopes.
followUp: Extend the temporary-index commit discipline to all governed commit surfaces in both repos (record-commit, git commit --task, planning-repo commit helpers), and add a doctor patrol that flags commits whose file set spans multiple task scopes.
